notes: commit=9cd4ca2d0a5c67d1123d84ee5a9c21a04c04c459; extraction_method=regex_best_effort; doc_density_composite=97.9; params are name-only placeholders (annotation='Any') -- EXP-1 classifier tests selection only, not call-correctness.

tools.name: check_matlab_code
tools.docstring: Perform static code analysis on a MATLAB script (`script_path`) using MATLAB's built-in Code Analyzer function in an existing MATLAB session. Returns warnings about coding style, potential errors, deprecated functions, performance issues, and best practice violations. It also includes information about where each issue occurs and how it can be fixed in MATLAB. This is a non-destructive, read-only operation that helps identify code quality issues without executing the script.

tools.name: detect_matlab_toolboxes
tools.docstring: Returns information about installed MATLAB and toolboxes, including version numbers.

tools.name: evaluate_matlab_code
tools.docstring: Evaluate a string of MATLAB code (`code`) in an existing MATLAB session. Optionally specify a project folder (`project_path`) to set as the current working folder before execution. Returns the command window output from code execution.

tools.name: run_matlab_file
tools.docstring: Execute a MATLAB script file (`script_path`) in an existing MATLAB session and capture its command window output. The script runs with the working folder automatically set to the script's location. The script must exist and be a valid .m file. Returns the command window output or a success message if no output is generated.

tools.name: run_matlab_test_file
tools.docstring: Execute a MATLAB test script (`script_path`) using MATLAB's built-in runtests function and return comprehensive test results. Designed specifically for MATLAB unit test files that follow MATLAB's testing framework conventions.

tools.name: eval_in_matlab_session
tools.docstring: Evaluate a string of MATLAB code (`code`) in an existing MATLAB session, given its session ID (`session_id`). Optionally specify a project folder (`project_path`) to set as the working folder before execution.

tools.name: list_available_matlabs
tools.docstring: List the installed MATLAB versions on the host and their root directories.

tools.name: start_matlab_session
tools.docstring: Starts a new MATLAB session for the provided MATLAB root (`matlab_root`) and returns a session ID (`session_id`).

tools.name: stop_matlab_session
tools.docstring: Stops an existing MATLAB session, given its session ID (`session_id`).

tools.name: matlab_coding_guidelines
tools.docstring: Provides comprehensive MATLAB coding standards for improving code readability, maintainability, and collaboration. The guidelines encompass naming conventions, formatting, commenting, performance optimization, and error handling.

tools.name: plain_text_live_code_guidelines
tools.docstring: Provides rules and guidelines for generating live scripts using the plain text Live Code `.m` file format, suitable for version control and AI-assisted development. Note that to run plain text live scripts you need MATLAB R2025a or newer. For details, see [Live Code File Format (MathWorks)](https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/matlab_prog/plain-text-file-format-for-live-scripts.html).
